name: 1st and Last Stop Financial
description_short: Credit repair and real estate financing company founded by Lily Garza offering credit disputes, owner-financed property sales, and financial coaching for clients with damaged credit histories.
description_long:
1st and Last Stop Financial was founded by Lily Garza, CEO and a member of NAHREP (National Association of Hispanic Real Estate Professionals). The company emerged from Garza's personal financial journey, including experiences with bankruptcy recovery and building credit from an ITIN background. Garza's motivation stemmed from observing financial inequities—specifically why some people qualify for 0% financing while others do not—and realizing that personal finance education is often passed down through families rather than taught formally in schools.

The company offers three primary service lines: credit repair (dispute errors and negative items on credit reports), real estate sales through owner financing, and financial consulting. Their credit repair services target consumers who want to make major purchases requiring credit checks, understand their credit health, or feel stuck at their current credit score. The owner-financing real estate option explicitly markets to those with poor credit, self-employed individuals, and those without SSN or ITIN documentation. All services include personalized consultations to develop customized financial plans.

What distinguishes 1st and Last Stop Financial is the integration of credit repair with real estate and owner financing—positioning themselves as a comprehensive solution for credit-challenged buyers seeking homeownership. Garza's personal credibility (having navigated bankruptcy, single motherhood, and credit rebuilding) is central to their marketing. They emphasize bilingual service (Spanish/English) and cultural alignment through NAHREP membership. The company specifically targets first-time homebuyers and individuals rejected by traditional mortgage lenders.

A straightforward assessment: This is a credit repair company with real estate and owner-financing divisions. While Garza's personal narrative is compelling, the website contains no third-party verification of specific credit repair results, turnaround timelines, dispute success rates, or pricing transparency. The owner-financing model may carry significant risk depending on contract terms. Consumers should verify licensure status, request case examples, understand fee structures, and review all financing agreements thoroughly before engaging.
address: 6640 Camp Bowie Boulevard, Fort Worth, T, suite 900B

company_info.headquarters: 6640 Camp Bowie Boulevard, Fort Worth, T, suite 900B

pros: Founder Lily Garza has documented personal experience with bankruptcy recovery and credit rebuilding, lending credibility to credit repair guidance
Offers owner-financing alternative for buyers rejected by traditional lenders, expanding homeownership access for credit-challenged consumers
Explicitly serves non-traditional credit profiles including self-employed, those without SSN/ITIN, and individuals with poor credit histories
Bilingual (English/Spanish) services and NAHREP board membership indicate focus on underserved Hispanic communities
Integrates credit repair with real estate and financial consulting into one service platform, positioning as comprehensive financial solution
Emphasizes personalized consultation and master financial planning rather than one-size-fits-all approach
No upfront fees mentioned for initial consultation, allowing free evaluation
cons: Website lacks specific pricing information, dispute timelines, success rates, or performance guarantees for credit repair services
No third-party credentials or licenses displayed; unclear whether company is licensed to conduct credit repairs in all operating states
Owner-financing terms, interest rates, and contract details are completely absent from website—significant transparency gap for real estate financing
Limited information on who comprises 'the team' beyond founder Garza; no staff credentials, licensing, or experience details provided
Website is primarily narrative-focused rather than results-oriented; no case studies, client testimonials, or statistical outcomes shared
best_for: First-time homebuyers with credit challenges who have been rejected by traditional mortgage lenders
Self-employed individuals or those without traditional employment documentation seeking owner-financed property purchases
Spanish-speaking consumers seeking bilingual credit repair and real estate services in Hispanic-focused communities
Individuals with specific obstacles (bankruptcy history, limited credit file, ITIN status) who relate to founder's personal story
diagnosis: 1st and Last Stop Financial is best for credit-challenged first-time homebuyers and self-employed individuals seeking alternatives to traditional lending, particularly Spanish-speaking consumers. The main caveat is complete lack of transparency on credit repair pricing, timelines, success rates, dispute outcomes, and owner-financing terms—consumers must request detailed information and verification of licensure before engagement.
services: Credit report analysis and credit health assessment
Credit dispute services for errors and negative items on credit reports
Credit score improvement consulting and financial guidance
Owner-financed real estate sales (all credit profiles accepted)
Mortgage pre-qualification and mortgage loan denial assistance
Self-employed and non-traditional income documentation support
Real estate property listings and viewings
Financing for borrowers without SSN or ITIN
